title: Fessia gorganica
taxonomy: Fessia gorganica is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Fessia. The classification of Fessia gorganica is within the kingdom Plantae, and its full classification is Fessia gorganica within the family Annonaceae and kingdom Plantae. Fessia gorganica's classification further specifies that it is within the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species Fessia gorganica is often classified within the class Magnoliopsida due to its characteristics.

intro: Fessia gorganica is a medicinal plant species native to the mountainous regions of Iran, belonging to the Apiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Fessia gorganica include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic compounds, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fessia gorganica is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of inflammatory conditions.
